name: rewst-buddy
description: Unofficial community VS Code extension for editing Rewst templates locally. Not affiliated with Rewst LLC.

languageModelTools.name: list_template_links
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List local files linked to Rewst templates (path, template name, template id, org).

languageModelTools.name: web_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the public web; returns result titles, URLs, and snippets.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_get
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read a Rewst workflow as a normalized graph: nodes (tasks with their action ref and input) and edges (transitions with their condition, label, target task names, and published context variables). Returns far less noise than raw GraphQL and the node/edge names this tool uses are exactly what buddy_workflow_edit operations expect. By default (detail "summary") it returns a concise ANALYSIS view: task ids, transition ids, canvas x/y positions, and the version token are OMITTED and tasks/edges are referenced by name — ideal for understanding what a workflow does with far fewer tokens, and enough to edit (operations resolve tasks by name). Pass detail "full" only when you need those ids/positions, e.g. to reposition a task or target one specific transition by its id. Use this before editing a workflow.

orgId.description: The id of the org that owns the workflow.

detail.description: "summary" (default): concise analysis view, no ids/positions/version token. "full": adds task ids, transition ids, and canvas positions for repositioning or targeting a specific transition.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Find Rewst workflows by name (or id) across every org you can access — the reliable way to resolve a workflow instead of guessing its id or paging through GraphQL. On first use it builds and CACHES an index of all workflows (id, name, org id, org name) reachable from your session — managed orgs and sub-orgs alike — then answers this and later searches from the cache with no re-listing. Pass query to match by name or id — matching ignores case, punctuation, and word order and requires every word, so "jon sandbox" finds "Jon's Sandbox" and "lock workflow" finds "[RAVEN] Workflow Lock". Workflows that match only because their ORG name matched are summarized separately (with the org id), so an org-name query never floods the list. orgId scopes to one org; limit caps results (default 25); refresh:true rebuilds the cache after workflows are created or renamed. Each result shows the workflow name, its id, and the ORG NAME (with org id) — feed those straight into buddy_workflow_get / buddy_workflow_edit / buddy_workflow_run.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against workflow name, id, and org name. Omit to list.

orgId.description: Restrict results to one org id.

refresh.description: Rebuild the cached index from the API before searching (default false).

languageModelTools.name: buddy_action_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Find Rewst actions for an org, or describe one action's inputs. Search mode (pass query) matches action name and ref, ranks core/common actions first, dedupes, and returns each match's ref, id, and category. Describe mode (pass ref or actionId) returns the action's parameters (the input schema you fill into a task's input) and output schema. Use describe mode before adding a task so you know which inputs the action accepts.

orgId.description: The org to search actions for.

query.description: Search term matched against action name and ref.

ref.description: Describe this exact action ref (e.g. core.noop).

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_edit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Edit a Rewst workflow by applying high-level operations. The tool reads the current workflow, applies the operations to the full graph, and saves it back with conflict detection and an undoable patch — you never resend the whole workflow or manage version tokens yourself. Operations (each an object with an "op" field): add_task {name, action (ref or id) OR subWorkflowId, input?, publishResultAs?, transitionMode?, join?, with?, x?, y?}; update_task {id|name, set:{...}}; delete_task {id|name} (also removes edges pointing at it); connect {from, to, when?, label?, publish?} (from/to are task names or ids); disconnect {from, to?|transitionId?}; set_transition {from, to?|transitionId?, set:{when?, label?, publish?, to?}}; reposition {task, x, y} (move a task to canvas coordinates); set_inputs {inputs: [{name, type?, title?, default?, description?, required?, multiline?}]} (replace the workflow's run/call inputs; an input default is a Jinja expression like "{{ false }}" or "{{ CTX.x }}" — raw booleans/numbers are wrapped for you). Define workflow inputs ONLY with set_inputs: it writes the input name list, the action parameters that actually drive the run/call form, and the inputSchema together. Do not put inputs in varsSchema, which is a separate variables map. To call another workflow as a sub-workflow, set subWorkflowId (or action) to that workflow's id — a workflow's id is its action id; there is no separate run-workflow action. To branch on what a task returned, read RESULT.<field> in that task's own outgoing transition conditions, or CTX.<alias>.<field> when the task sets publishResultAs to <alias>; a task's or sub-workflow's internally published variables are NOT in this workflow's CTX. when defaults to "{{ SUCCEEDED }}"; the tool automatically orders each task's transitions so custom conditions come before the "{{ SUCCEEDED }}" success catch-all (with FOLLOW_FIRST a success transition placed first would shadow every custom condition after it, so the custom Jinja would never evaluate). The tool also writes a safe default transitionMode (FOLLOW_FIRST) and join (1) on any task missing them, so you only set transitionMode/join when you want a non-default (transitionMode "FOLLOW_ALL" for a parallel fan-out, or join for a real join/merge). A new task is positioned on the canvas below the action it is connected from (leaving a gap) unless you pass x/y; x is canvas right, y is down, in free pixels. This is a mutation: it MUST include workflowId, workflowName, orgId, orgName (get them from buddy_workflow_get) and requires user approval each time it runs.

workflowName.description: The workflow name, shown in the approval prompt.

orgId.description: The id of the org that owns the workflow.

orgName.description: The org name, shown in the approval prompt.

operations.description: Ordered list of edit operations to apply.

comment.description: Optional patch comment describing the change.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_autolayout
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Auto-arrange a Rewst workflow: recompute every task position into a clean top-down layout (each task one layer below the actions that lead to it, laid left-to-right with spacing), then save. Use this to tidy a messy or programmatically built workflow, or after adding several tasks. This is a mutation: it MUST include workflowId, workflowName, orgId, orgName (get them from buddy_workflow_get) and requires user approval each time it runs. For positioning a single task, use buddy_workflow_edit with a reposition operation instead.

workflowId.description: The workflow id to lay out.

workflowName.description: The workflow name, shown in the approval prompt.

orgId.description: The id of the org that owns the workflow.

orgName.description: The org name, shown in the approval prompt.

comment.description: Optional patch comment describing the change.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_render_jinja
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a Jinja template against a real workflow execution's context and return only the result. Use this to CONFIRM a transition condition, task input, or publish expression evaluates the way you expect BEFORE editing a workflow — the agent otherwise guesses wrong (e.g. comparing a boolean to the string 'true', or reading a sub-workflow result from CTX.<field> instead of CTX.<publishResultAs>.<field>). Pass executionId and the tool fetches that run's context server-side, so the (large) context never enters the chat; or pass vars as an ad-hoc context object. This renders against the STORED context snapshot, which is the CTX namespace only — the live runtime objects WORKFLOW, ORG, USER, and RESULT do NOT exist here, so use their CTX equivalents: the execution id is CTX.execution_id, the org id is CTX.organization.id, and the running workflow's own id is CTX.trigger_instance.trigger.workflow_id. To discover what a run actually holds, pass keys:true to list the context's top-level keys instead of rendering (then drill in with {{ CTX.<key> }}). In the template, CTX is the context: read a field as {{ CTX.field }}, and to dump the whole context use {{ CTX() }} with parentheses — in a live Rewst workflow CTX is callable, so bare {{ CTX }} does not work. By default the last context snapshot of the run is used; contextIndex picks another. Returns the rendered value, or the Jinja error if it fails.

orgId.description: The org the template renders in.

template.description: The Jinja to evaluate, e.g. "{{ CTX.learning_result.proceed | d(false) }}". Omit when keys is true.

executionId.description: A workflow execution id; its context is fetched server-side and used as CTX (kept out of the chat).

vars.description: Ad-hoc context object to render against instead of an execution.

contextIndex.description: Which context snapshot of the execution to use (default: the last/most-complete).

keys.description: List the context's top-level keys instead of rendering a template (default false).

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Trigger a run of a Rewst workflow (via testWorkflow) — to test a workflow end to end or kick it off for another purpose. Pass input as the workflow's run inputs (the parameters from buddy_workflow_get's workflow.inputs). By default the tool WAITS for the run to finish and reports the final status; if it failed it automatically includes the failing task's log (status, message, input, result) so you see the cause in one call without a separate buddy_execution_logs round-trip. Pass wait:false to return immediately with just the execution id. The execution id is included either way; feed it to buddy_execution_logs or buddy_render_jinja to dig further. This actually executes the workflow's automation, so it requires user approval each time it runs.

workflowName.description: The workflow name, shown in the approval prompt.

orgId.description: The id of the org that owns the workflow.

orgName.description: The org name, shown in the approval prompt.

input.description: The workflow's run inputs (maps input name to value).

wait.description: Wait for the run to finish and report its outcome (default true). False returns immediately.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_workflow_executions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List a workflow's recent executions, most recent first — typically to find recent FAILED runs to debug. Pass status to filter (e.g. "failed", "succeeded", "running"; lowercase). Returns each execution's id, status, time, and successful-task count. Feed a failed execution's id to buddy_render_jinja (executionId) to inspect the context it produced and see why a condition or expression went wrong.

workflowId.description: The workflow whose executions to list.

orgId.description: The id of the org that owns the workflow.

status.description: Filter by execution status, lowercase (e.g. "failed", "succeeded", "running"). Omit for any.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_execution_logs
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect one workflow execution's task logs: per task, its status, and for failed tasks the message, the input it received, and the result it produced — the fastest way to see WHY a run failed, instead of hand-writing taskLogs GraphQL. Get an executionId from buddy_workflow_run or buddy_workflow_executions. By default every task shows name + status and failed tasks additionally show message, input, and result (truncated); pass includeResult to include every task's result, or failedOnly to list only failed tasks. A task's input shows exactly what it received (an empty-string id means the caller passed nothing); its result shows the real output shape — read it before assuming a wrapper key (e.g. some actions return a list directly, not { items: [...] }).

executionId.description: The workflow execution id to inspect.

includeResult.description: Include every task's result, not just failed tasks' (default false).

languageModelTools.name: buddy_graphql_schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Inspect the Rewst GraphQL schema with the user session before composing operations. With no args, lists root Query/Mutation/Subscription fields. Use typeName to inspect fields/input fields/enum values for one type. Use search to find matching type names and root operation fields.

search.description: Find matching type names and root operation fields.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_approval
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Internal: approves a Rewst-side action Cage-Free Rewsty paused for. Emitted by the Cage-Free Rewsty chat model itself — never request this tool.

argsPreview.description: Human-readable preview of the tool arguments.

resume.description: The original request to re-send once approved.

languageModelTools.name: buddy_graphql
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a GraphQL operation against the user's Rewst instance with their session. Prefer buddy_graphql_schema first when you are unsure about field names or arguments. Queries run directly and return JSON data. Mutations require the user's approval and MUST include four identifying fields: scopeId and scopeName (the id and human-readable name of the single resource the mutation changes, e.g. a workflow's id and name) plus orgId and orgName (the id and name of the org it runs in). A mutation missing any of them is refused. Every mutation is confirmed each time it runs; the four fields name the resource and org in the approval prompt so the user can recognize what is changing. Subscriptions are not supported.

scopeId.description: Required for mutations: a stable id of the resource being changed (e.g. the workflow id), shown in the approval prompt.

scopeName.description: Required for mutations: the resource's human-readable name (e.g. the workflow name), shown to the user in the approval prompt.

orgId.description: Required for mutations: the id of the org the mutation runs in.

orgName.description: Required for mutations: the name of the org the mutation runs in, shown in the approval prompt.

rewst-buddy.regions.description: Regional configuration for different Rewst instances

loginUrl.description: Login page URL for the region

subscriptionsUrl.description: GraphQL subscriptions WebSocket URL (optional; derived from graphqlUrl when omitted)

rewst-buddy.syncOnSaveByDefault.description: When enabled, all linked files sync on save by default (use Disable Sync-On-Save to exclude specific files). When disabled, files only sync when explicitly enabled.

rewst-buddy.autoFetchOnOpen.description: When enabled, automatically fetch remote template updates when opening linked files. This only applies changes if the local file hasn't been modified since the last sync.

rewst-buddy.server.enabled.description: Enable the local HTTP server to receive session tokens from browser extensions

rewst-buddy.server.port.description: Port number for the local HTTP server (default: 27121)

rewst-buddy.server.host.description: Host address to bind the server (default: 127.0.0.1 for localhost-only access)

rewst-buddy.ai.conversationType.description: Conversation type sent to the Rewst AI assistant (HELP_DOCS answers documentation questions)

rewst-buddy.ai.customInstructions.description: Standing instructions prepended to every message sent to the Rewst AI assistant (e.g. preferred answer style, your environment details). Note: this is sent as part of your message — it cannot override Rewst's own system prompt.

rewst-buddy.ai.showActivity.description: Show Cage-Free Rewsty's live activity (documentation searches and tool calls) as it works, instead of only a spinner until the answer arrives.

enumDescriptions: Workspace context: a compact workspace overview plus the list of files linked to Rewst templates.
Web search: let Cage-Free Rewsty search the public web (private/loopback hosts are always blocked).
GraphQL: compose and run GraphQL operations against your Rewst instance using your session (mutations require your approval).
Workflows: read and edit Rewst workflows, search actions, and render Jinja against executions (edits require your approval).

rewst-buddy.ai.tools.markdownDescription: Extra AI tools Cage-Free Rewsty may use — check the ones to enable. `workspace` shares a workspace overview and the template-link list; `web`, `graphql`, and `workflows` let the assistant act against the web or your Rewst instance, where GraphQL and workflow **edits always require your approval**.

commands.title: Rewst Buddy: Link File to Template from URL
Rewst Buddy: Link File to Template
Rewst Buddy: Open Template from URL
Rewst Buddy: Resume Rewst AI Conversation
Rewst Buddy: Apply Rewst AI Suggestion

explorer/context.when: rewst-buddy.anyActiveSessions && explorerResourceIsFolder && resourcePath not in rewst-buddy.linkedFolders

scripts.codegen:check: npm run codegen && git diff --exit-code src/sessions/graphql/sdk.ts || (echo 'SDK is out of sync! Run: npm run codegen' && exit 1)
scripts.test:grep:integration: npm run compile:test && cross-env REWST_TEST_INTEGRATION=1 vscode-test --grep
scripts.release:tag: git tag v$npm_package_version && git push origin v$npm_package_version
